metadata.summary: A collection of common tools for Scoota services.

documentation:
The library provides a collection of tools and functionality for the Scoota Platform services.

![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/rbx.svg)

## Installation

Install this library in a virtualenv using pip.

```
pip install rbx
```

> See the [wiki](https://github.com/rockabox/rbx/wiki) for more details on the contents of this library.

# Development

To contribute to this project, install and run `tox`:

```
pip install -r tests/setup.txt
tox -m local
```

classes.name: rbx.gcp.cloud_tasks.Queue

methods.name: rbx.gcp.cloud_tasks.Queue.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the Google Cloud Queue.

Parameters:
    project_id (str):
        The Google Cloud Project ID.
    location (str):
        The location code of the Queue.
        This must match the location of the App Engine application.
    queue_name (str):
        The name of the queue.
    service:
        The default App Engine service that the task will be sent to.
        By default, tasks are sent to the 'default' service.

methods.name: rbx.gcp.cloud_tasks.Queue.retry
documentation:
Exponential retry decorator.

This class is a decorator used to add exponential back-off retry behavior to the
create_task RPC call.

We implement it here as a property of the queue, first to always use the same retry logic,
and second to allow us to mock it more easily in tests.

methods.name: rbx.gcp.cloud_tasks.Queue.enqueue
documentation:
Create a task in the queue.

This will create an App Engine HTTP request. The task will be delivered to the App Engine
app which belongs to the same project as the queue.

Parameters:
    uri (str):
        The url that the task will be sent to.
    headers (dict):
        Optional additional HTTP request headers.
        This map contains the header field names and values.
    method (str):
        The HTTP method to use for the request. Can be either GET or POST.
        The default is POST.
    payload (bytes):
        HTTP request body.
        A request body is allowed only if the HTTP method is POST.
        The payload must be encoded.
    service:
        The App Engine service that the task will be sent to.
        By default, the task is sent to the service implemented by the Queue.

Raises:
    TypeError: If the payload parameter is not a bytes type.
Utility class to create queues and tasks in Google Cloud Tasks.

functions.name: rbx.gcp.pubsub.strip
documentation: Strip whitespaces from the message payload.

classes.name: rbx.gcp.pubsub.PubSubPublisher

methods.name: rbx.gcp.pubsub.PubSubPublisher._format
documentation: Format a message item into a Pub/Sub message.

methods.name: rbx.gcp.pubsub.PubSubPublisher._publish
documentation:
Publish to Pub/Sub.

We wait for the result to ensure the publish operation has succeeded. The 30 seconds
timeout is set in case something goes horribly wrong, which would lock the thread forever.

Note that the Pub/Sub message must be sent as a bytestring.

methods.name: rbx.gcp.pubsub.PubSubPublisher.dispatch
documentation:
Dispatch events to be published.

The Pub/Sub library takes care of batching behind the hook, so all we
need to do is post the messages independently.
Publish messages to Pub/Sub.

classes.name: rbx.gcp.storage.Storage

methods.name: rbx.gcp.storage.Storage.list_objects
documentation:
Return an iterator of all objects in the bucket matching the prefix.

The objects are returned as bytes.
Provides functionality to upload/download content to/from GCS.

classes.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.__init__
documentation:
The name may include the bucket prefix.

e.g.: bucket-name/path/to/dir/

In which case the prefix instance attribute will be automatically prefixed to all keys.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.clone
documentation: Clone the given S3 key to the location given in 'dest'.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.copy
documentation:
Copy a file to the given S3 key.

The filename will be read, so filename is expected to be the path to the file itself.

When download is True, we add the "ContentDisposition: attachment" header so that it makes
browsers download the file when accessed directly.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.create
documentation:
Create the S3 Bucket.

Only create the bucket if it does not already exist.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.delete
documentation: Delete the S3 Bucket.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.delete_keys
documentation:
Delete keys from the S3 Bucket

Will delete all keys in the Bucket given a key unless you specify a prefix, then it will
only delete files which start with that particular prefix.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.exists
documentation: Check whether the Bucket exists in S3.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.get_key
documentation:
Check whether the given key exists in the bucket.
Will return the object or None.

methods.name: rbx.aws.s3.S3Bucket.save
documentation:
Save a file-like object to the given S3 key.

When download is True, we add the "ContentDisposition: attachment" header so that it makes
browsers download the file when accessed directly.

An AWS S3 Bucket.

Wraps the boto3.Bucket class to provide a higher level of abstraction, as needed by the
Platform.

The main purpose of the create() and delete() method is to aid integration testing.
We therefore skip any CORS or permission setting when creating buckets.
In a production environment, these buckets are expected to already exist, and to have been
configured accordingly.

The only scenario where endpoint_url is set is in testing and development. In a normal
setting, this should not be set.

modules.name: rbx.exceptions
documentation: Common RBX exceptions.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.FatalException
documentation:
Raise this exception for non-transient errors.

These exceptions can be given an extra details object, which may be used by the caller to log
this as extra information.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.HTTPException
documentation: A FatalException with ani optional HTTP status code and a URL.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.AWSException
documentation: Raised when an unexpected error occurs using boto3.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.BadRequest
documentation:
Convenience Exception to use from within a Flask service/blueprint.

Raising this exception will be caught by the error handler and formatted as an HTTP response,
using the provided status_code.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.ClientException
documentation:
Raised from within the rbx.clients package, caused by third-party APIs.

This exception is fatal and considered non-transient.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.ClientError
documentation: 4xx client errors.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.ServerError
documentation: 5xx server errors.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.TransientServerError
documentation: Transient 5xx server errors.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.RetryError
documentation:
Raised when a function has exhausted all of its available retries.

Parameters:
    message (str):
        The exception message.
    cause (Exception):
        The last exception raised when retring the function.

classes.name: rbx.exceptions.TransientException
documentation:
Raise this exception when we know the cause is transient (e.g.: connection errors,
consistency error, ...).

functions.name: rbx.auth.decorators.protect
documentation:
Protect an endpoint via API Keys.

If with_grants is True, the Grants object is added to the caller as a 'grants' keyword
argument.

When DEV_MODE is enabled, all restrictions are lifted.

Keys are retrieved from the Keystore, and cached using the LRU local caching.
They are provided via the "X-RBX-TOKEN" header, and can also be provided via the 'key'
query string parameter.

If the request originates from App Engine's Cron Service, or from Cloud Tasks, it is always
granted access.

From the documentation:

    The X-Appengine-Cron header is set internally by Google App Engine.
    If your request handler finds this header it can trust that the request is a cron request.
    The X- headers are stripped by App Engine when they originate from external sources so that
    you can trust this header.

classes.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore._inventory
documentation: Given a DocumentSnapshot, return the fully loaded Inventory object.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore._key
documentation: Given a DocumentSnapshot, return the fully loaded Key object.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore.add_campaign
documentation: Add a campaign to the campaigns inventory.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore.add_campaigns
documentation: Add campaigns to the campaigns inventory.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore.create_key
documentation:
Make a new API key.

A key is associated with a user via her email address. If a key already exists for that
user, the key is updated instead.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore.get_campaigns
documentation: List all campaigns in the inventory.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore.get_inventory
documentation: Retrieve a key from the inventory.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore.get_key
documentation: Retrieve a key by ID or email.

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Keystore.update_key
documentation: Set the new Key values in Firestore.
Manages API Keys stored in Google Cloud Firestore (Native Mode).

methods.name: rbx.auth.keystore.Key.has_access
documentation: Check whether the service and operation are granted access by this Key.

methods.name: rbx.queues.scheduler.Scheduler.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the Job Scheduler.

Parameters:
    queue (rbx.queue.Queue):
        The queue scheduled jobs will be submitted to.
    interval (int):
        How often the scheduler checks for new jobs to add to the queue (in seconds, can
        be floating-point for more precision).
        Defaults to 60 seconds.

methods.name: rbx.queues.scheduler.Scheduler.register_death
documentation:
Extend what is executed when death is coming.

More specifically, make sure all scheduled jobs are cancelled.
They will be re-created upon re-birth.

methods.name: rbx.queues.scheduler.Scheduler.register_jobs_from_settings
documentation: Given a set of scheduled jobs, register them.

modules.name: rbx.queues.jobs
documentation: Helper module to represent scheduled jobs.

classes.name: rbx.queues.jobs.JobSpec
documentation:
Represents a job spec.

This is a Value Object, and doesn't convey any logic.
All this does is some very limited field checking.

classes.name: rbx.queues.jobs.Jobs

methods.name: rbx.queues.jobs.Jobs.from_dict
documentation:
Constructor that creates a Jobs instance from a dictionary.
The dict is expected to have be valid and have the following format:

```pycon
>>> {
>>>     'job_name': {
>>>         'task': 'full.path.to.method',
>>>         'args': (1, 'arg2'),
>>>         'kwargs': {'kw1': 'value1'}
>>>         'schedule': 'CRON expression',
>>>         'timeout': 180,
>>>     },
>>>     ...
>>> }
```
Iterable of JobSpecs.

functions.name: rbx.queues.retry
documentation:
Decorator used to move a failing job back into the queue.

Parameters:
    exceptions (Exception):
        An exception type, or a tuple of types, used to determine whether, when raised from
        within a job, the method should be considered retryable.

        e.g.:
        >>> retry(exceptions=ValueError)
        >>> retry(exceptions=(TransientError, ValueError))

    max_retries (int):
        The number of attempts before giving up. Defaults to 3.

classes.name: rbx.queues.Queue

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the Job Queue.

Parameters:
    name (str):
        An optional queue name. Defaults to "default".

    host (str):
        The Redis cluster host.
        Uses '0.0.0.0' by default.
    port (int):
        The Redis cluster port.
        Uses port 6379 by default.
    db (int):
        The Redis cluster db.
        Uses 0 by default.
    module:
        The default Queue uses either a method, or as string reference to an importable
        method.
        For string references, this paramater can be used to always load the job method
        from a specific module.

        >>> queue = Queue(module='src.tasks')
        >>> queue.enqueue('method')
        Job(function='src.tasks.method', ...)

    conn (StrictRedis):
        A redis connection may be provided instead of the host/port/db combination.
    is_async (bool):
        Set to True to skip the workers and perform the job instantly.
    no_delay (bool):
        Use this flag to cancel the scheduled/delaying feature, and enqueue a delayed job
        immediately. Useful for testing purposes.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.exception_handlers
documentation: The handlers must be listed in order of expected execution.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue._move_to_failed_queue
documentation: This handler moves the job to the failed queue.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue._requeue_job
documentation:
This handler does the requeuing, according to the 'failures' meta data set by the
previous handler.

If the retry is given a sequence of exception types, the method will only be retried if the
exception raised is of one of these types.

When the maximum retry attempts has been reached, we make sure to clear the 'failures'
flag, and move the job to the next handler in the stack (which will move it to the failed
queue).

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue._retry_on_failure
documentation:
This handler flags jobs which are retry-able ('retry' = True) by incrementing the
'failures' meta data.

It will then pass on the handling of the requeuing itself to the next handler in the stack.
If we were to requeue the job at this stage, what would happen is the job would be queued
up before the next handler had the chance to fallback to the next, ending in an infinite
loop of failing jobs with the same ID.

The retry() decorator is provided to flag any method as retry-able.

Also note that scheduled jobs do never get retried, and skip the failed queue entirely.
They will get retried at the next run anyway.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.as_sync
documentation: Return a Queue instance with the is_async flag set to False.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.cancel_job
documentation: Cancel the given job.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.cancel_jobs
documentation: Cancel of bunch of jobs given their IDs.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.empty
documentation: Empty all queues.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.enqueue
documentation:
Register a job to be processed.

Parameters:
    function (str|function):
        A function, or a string reference to a function. If self.module is set, it is used
        as the default location for looking up method names.
    enqueue_in (int):
        By default, jobs are scheduled to be executed as soon as a worker is available. The
        execution can be further delayed by specifying how to long to wait. The value is
        expressed in seconds.
    args (tuple):
        The positional arguments to pass on to the function.
    kwargs (dict):
        The keyword arguments to pass on to the function.

Returns:
    The job to be executed.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.fetch_job
documentation: Fetch a job for a Job ID.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.get_jobs
documentation: Get all jobs.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.get_scheduled_jobs
documentation: Get all scheduled jobs.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.get_worker
documentation: Return an instance of a worker.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.requeue_failed_job
documentation: Requeue a failed job to its original queue.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.requeue_failed_jobs
documentation: Requeue of bunch of failed jobs given their IDs.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.requeue_job
documentation:
Requeue a job that is due to be re-tried after a failure.

These jobs are pushed back into the queue without having made it to the failed queue.

The requeue_job method provided by the Queue class does not keep the meta data, which we
use to check how often the job has been tried. We therefore requeue these jobs by enqueing
the original signature, including the meta data and the original Job ID.

methods.name: rbx.queues.Queue.with_no_delay
documentation: Return a Queue instance with the no_delay flag set to True.
The Queue provides an interface to farm out jobs to be processed asynchronously.

functions.name: rbx.web.handlers.register_error_handlers
documentation:
Register handlers for Exceptions.

Any exception, whether raised manually or unexpected, will be caught and returned as a
JSON-formatted response.

We catch any code-specific Exceptions, namely custom validation (400) and uncaught
errors (500).

Custom validation should be raised in the app using the BadRequest class:

```pycon
>>> raise BadRequest('Message')
```

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.image.login
documentation: Login to the Amazon Container Registry.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.image.build
documentation:
Build the docker image.

Note that parameters always take precedence over environment variables.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.image.upload
documentation:
Upload the docker image to Amazon Container Registry.

Needless to say, this image must have been already built.

Note that parameters always take precedence over environment variables.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.apprunner.submit
documentation: Submit a local Docker build and push the image to AWS ECR.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.apprunner.deploy
documentation: Deploy an image to App Runner.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.misc.clean
documentation: Remove all intermediate files from the source tree.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.misc.get_next_tag
documentation:
Generate tag from Git.

A helper to generate the next tag based on the current git tag.

To use in Jenkins job:

    >>> export VERSION=`buildtools misc git-next-tag`

Expects an integer, otherwise will start at 1.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.misc.get_merge_desc
documentation:
Get description from Git.

A helper to get the description of a merge commit.

To use in a Jenkins job:

    >>> export DESCRIPTION=`buildtools misc git-merge-desc`

When merging a PR the subject line will be added to the body of the merge
commit.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.misc.get_npm_version
documentation:
Get version from NPM.

A helper to get the npm package version from a package.json file.

To use in Jenkins job:

    >>> export VERSION=`buildtools misc npm-version`

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.tasks.ec2.deploy
documentation:
Deploy to EC2.

Download a Docker image and start the container on a Remote Server.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.cli.cli
documentation: Scoota (a.k.a. RBX) buildtools.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.cli.apprunner_group
documentation: Manage AppRunner services.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.cli.image_group
documentation: Manage images on Amazon Container Registry.

functions.name: rbx.buildtools.cli.misc_group
documentation: Miscellaneous tools.

functions.name: rbx.utils.mdm.notify_mdm
documentation: Send an event to Google Pub/Sub to notify the change.

modules.name: rbx.utils
documentation: Common utility methods.

functions.name: rbx.utils.smart_url

classes.name: rbx.utils.Singleton
documentation: Metaclass providing a thread-safe Singleton interface.

classes.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.auth
documentation: The Reporting Service uses Digest Authentication.

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.create_schedule
documentation:
Create a scheduled job.

Return the job_id of the job just created.

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.delete_schedule
documentation: Create the scheduled job.

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.pause_schedule
documentation: Pause the scheduled job.

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.resume_schedule
documentation: Resume the scheduled job.

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.update_schedule
documentation: Update the scheduled job.

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.get_report
documentation:
Retrieve report data using the specified parameters.

Parameters:
    breakdowns (list):
        The list of requested breakdowns.
    metrics (list):
        The list of requested metrics.
    custom_metrics (dict):
        A keyed object detailing the labels and units of any custom metrics.
        The key must be the position of the custom metric in the `metrics` parameter,
        beginning with `0`, as a string.

        By default, custom metrics are labelled `N/A` and use the `number` unit.
    campaign (int):
        The id of the Campaign.
    anomalies (str):
        The report anomalies flag is used to determine which variant of the metric to
         return: exluding anomalies, including anomalies, or anomalies only.

        Valid values for this flag are: `excluded`, `included`, and `only`.
    filters (list[breakdown,operator,value]):
        Filter results on the given Breakdown having the value matching the given operator.
        Supported operators are: `eq`, `neq`, `in`, `nin`, and `like`
    date_range (string):
        A standard date range.
        One of `up_to_yesterday`, `campaign_life`, `today`, `yesterday`, `last_N_days`,
        `last_N_weeks`, `last_full_week`, or `last_full_month`.

        If omitted, all data up to yesterday midnight returned.
    start_date (string):
        The start date and time in ISO 8601 format.

        If omitted, the start date defaults to the oldest date on record.
        For a single campaign, that would be the first time the campaign received an event.
    end_date (string):
        The end date and time in ISO 8601 format.

        If omitted, the end date defaults to yesterday midnight.
    format (string):
        Specify what format the results should be returned in.
        Available formats are `csv`, `chartjs`, `tabular`, and `xlsx`.

        By default results are returned in JSON `tabular` format.
    step (int):
        The number of results to return. Use to limit the number of rows.

        By default all rows are returned.
    start (int):
        Use alongside `step` to fetch a specific set of results.
        `start` should be the number of the expected first result on the requested page.

        e.g.: using a `step` of 24, to get page 2, use `step=24, start=25`
    start (int):
        Use alongside `step` to fetch a specific set of results.
        `start` should be the number of the expected first result on the requested page.

        e.g.: using a `step` of 24, to get page 2, use `step=24, start=25`
    order_by (list[item,order]):
        Specify how the response should be ordered.

        The `order_by` parameter is expected to be a list of `item: sort` dictionary,
        where `item` is any Breakdown or Metric, and `sort` is either `ASC` or `DESC`.
    time_series (bool):
        Whether to return a time series.
    timezone (str):
        The time zone name of the request,
        If omitted, the time zone is assumed to be UTC.

Returns:
    Dict.
    A dict response containing the "headers" and "rows", with each row ordered to match the
    order of the headers.

methods.name: rbx.clients.reporting.ReportingClient.get_traffic_facets
documentation:
Retrieve traffic facets using the specified parameters.

Parameters:

    filters (list[breakdown,operator,value]):
        Filter results on the given Breakdown having the value matching the given operator.
        Supported operators are: `eq`, `neq`, `in`, `nin`, and `like`

    date_range (string):
        A standard relative dat range, in the form N{mhd}.
        Where `N` is the number of minutes (`m`), hours (`h`), or days (`d`), until now.

    start_date (string):
        The start date and time in ISO 8601 format.

    end_date (string):
        The end date and time in ISO 8601 format.

    timezone (str):
        The time zone name of the request,
        If omitted, the time zone is assumed to be UTC.

Returns:
    Dict.
    A dict response containing the facets names and their values list.
API Client for the Reporting Service.

classes.name: rbx.clients.oxr.ExchangeRate
documentation: Represents an exchange rate published at a specific time.

classes.name: rbx.clients.oxr.OXRClient

methods.name: rbx.clients.oxr.OXRClient.latest
documentation:
Get the latest exchange rates available from the Open Exchange Rates API.

Note that all rates are based on USD and this base currency is also given in the rates
object by default.

Usage:
\>>> client = OXRClient(app_id='92db86.........')
\>>> client.latest(['GBP', 'EUR'])
[\<ExchangeRate (EUR: 0.876002)>, \<ExchangeRate (GBP: 0.788209)>, \<ExchangeRate (USD: 1)>]

methods.name: rbx.clients.oxr.OXRClient._get
documentation:
Get currency exchange rates.

The default base currency of the Open Exchange Rates API is US Dollars (USD).

A simple HTTP client to retrieve currency exchange rates.

See https://docs.openexchangerates.org/docs/latest-json for the implementation doc.

modules.name: rbx.clients.retry
documentation:
Helpers for retrying functions with exponential back-off.

The `Retry` decorator can be used to retry functions that raise exceptions using exponential
backoff. The retry is limited by a `deadline`. The deadline is the maxmimum amount of time a
method can block.

This decorator will retry transient exceptions.

```pycon
>>> @retry.Retry()
>>> def call_method():
>>>     return client.method()
```

functions.name: rbx.clients.retry.if_exception_type
documentation:
Creates a predicate to check if the exception is of a given type.

Parameters:
    exception_types (Sequence[Exception]):
        The exception types to check for.
Returns:
    Callable[Exception]:
        A predicate that returns True if the provided exception is of the given type(s).

functions.name: rbx.clients.retry.exponential_sleep_generator
documentation:
Generates sleep intervals based on the exponential back-off algorithm.

This implements the `Truncated Exponential Back-off`_ algorithm:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exponential_backoff

Parameters:
    initial (float):
        The minimum amount of time to delay. This must be greater than 0.
    maximum (float):
        The maximum amount of time to delay.
    multiplier (float):
        The multiplier applied to the delay.

Yields:
    float: successive sleep intervals.

functions.name: rbx.clients.retry.retry_target
documentation:
Call a function and retry if it fails.

This is the lowest-level retry helper. Generally, you'll use the
higher-level retry helper {class}`Retry`.

Parameters:
: target(Callable):

  : The function to call and retry.

  predicate (Callable[Exception]):

  : A callable used to determine if an exception raised by the target should be considered
    retryable. It should return True to retry or False otherwise.

  sleep_generator (Iterable[float]):

  : An infinite iterator that determines how long to sleep between retries.

  deadline (float):

  : How long to keep retrying the target. The last sleep period is shortened as necessary,
    so that the last retry runs at `deadline` (and not considerably beyond it).

  on_error (Callable[Exception]):

  : A function to call while processing a retryable exception. Any error raised by this
    function will *not* be caught.

Returns:

: Any: the return value of the target function.

Raises:

: rbx.exceptions.RetryError: If the deadline is exceeded while retrying.
  ValueError: If the sleep generator stops yielding values.
  Exception: If the target raises a method that isn't retryable.

variables.name: rbx.clients.retry._DEFAULT_INITIAL_DELAY
documentation: seconds

variables.name: rbx.clients.retry._DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_DELAY
documentation: seconds

variables.name: rbx.clients.retry._DEFAULT_DEADLINE
documentation: seconds

classes.name: rbx.clients.retry.Retry

methods.name: rbx.clients.retry.Retry.__call__
documentation:
Wrap a callable with retry behavior.

Parameters:

: func (Callable): The callable to add retry behavior to.
  on_error (Callable[Exception]): A function to call while processing

  > a retryable exception. Any error raised by this function will
  > *not* be caught.

Returns:
: Callable: A callable that will invoke `func` with retry

  : behavior.

Exponential retry decorator.

This class is a decorator used to add exponential back-off retry behavior.

Parameters:
: predicate (Callable[Exception]):

  : A callable that should return `True` if the given exception is retryable.

  initial (float):

  : The minimum a,out of time to delay in seconds. This must be greater than 0.

  maximum (float):

  : The maximum amout of time to delay in seconds.

  multiplier (float):

  : The multiplier applied to the delay.

  deadline (float):

  : How long to keep retrying in seconds. The last sleep period is shortened as necessary,
    so that the last retry runs at `deadline` (and not considerably beyond it).

methods.name: rbx.clients.Client.auth
documentation:
Authentication method.

Returns None by default, meaning no authentication.
One can include Digest Authentication using the HttpAuth object, e.g.:

```pycon
>>> return HttpAuth(self.token, key='X-AUTH')
```

documentation: Wrap the method call in common error handling.

methods.name: rbx.clients.Client.request
documentation:
Attempt to re-authenticate when the token has expired.

Note that the Client must be logged in first for this to work.

Base Client offering basic functionality, such as logging in, auto-login on expiry, and
common error handling.

The Client is a JSON client, meaning it is to be used with an API that supports JSON format
in its requests and responses.

This class must be extended, do not use it directly.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.auth
documentation: adsquare uses Digest Authentication.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.create_config
documentation:
Create a new observation configuration.

The start and end date are expected to be in ISO 8601, though any format understood by
Arrow will work.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.disable_webhook
documentation: Disable the registered webhook for the given configuration ID.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.download_config
documentation:
Download the list of site locations uploaded to the given configuration.

The points are returned as a dictionary of (lat, long) tuples keyed by assigned ID.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.get_segments
documentation: Get segments from local cache.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.list_segments
documentation:
List all segments available.

According to the documentation, there is no pagination on this endpoint, so we expect the
full list to be returned with a single call.

See: https://docs.adsquare.com/#!/enrichmentMetaApi/getEnrichmentAudiences_1

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.pause_config
documentation: Pause the given configuration.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.register_webhook
documentation:
Register a Feedback URL webhook with adsquare for the given configuration ID.

The same method can be used to re-enable an existing/disabled webhook.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.resume_config
documentation: Resume the given configuration.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.store_segments
documentation:
Retrieve the list of segments, and then cache the results on a shelf.

As no caching mechanism exists on the API, we simply override the content of the cache
with the results from the list_segments() call.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.update_config
documentation:
Update the given configuration ID.

Only active and start/end dates can be changed.

methods.name: rbx.clients.adsquare.AdsquareClient.upload_config
documentation:
Upload a list of site locations to the given configuration.

The points is expected to be a list of (lat, long) tuples as floating values.

Client for adsquare API.

The API documentation can be found on the Platform wiki:
https://github.com/rockabox/rbx/wiki/adsquare

If the Client is already authenticated, the login process will be skipped.

documentation: Retrieve access token and refresh token from Broadsign with previous refresh token.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.create_advertiser
documentation: Create an Advertiser object.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.create_creative

Create a Creative object.

Note that the 'original_url' requires the protocol, or the API request will fail.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.get_advertiser
documentation: Retrieve an Advertiser object by name.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.get_category
documentation: Retrieve a Category object by code.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.get_creative
documentation:
Retrieve a Creative object by ID.

Passing the as_entity flag as True will return the object as returned by the system.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.get_publishers
documentation:
Return the full list of publishers.

This method is cached for 1 day.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient._get_publishers
documentation:
Retrieve a list of Publisher objects at the given page.

As the API restrict the response to 100 items, we need to repeat the process until we have
reached the last page.

The list may be further refined by ad_type, one of 'html', 'static', or 'video'.
The API does not allow filtering by query string, so we have to resort to filtering the
final results ourselves.

Note that when a publisher has no ad types defined (i.e.: 'allowed_ad_types': []), we
include the publisher. According to Broadsign, this happens when the details on the
publisher haven't made it to the API yet, so we should add them.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.get_screen
documentation:
Retrieve a page of screens from Broadsign

Parameters:
    last modified: date
        Restrict the results to panels entered past the specified date.
    page: int
        The page number of the panels to retrieve.

Returns:
    tuple: a tuple containing a list of screens retrieved from broadsign, and the next page.
    If there are no further pages, page will be set to -1.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.get_screens
documentation:
Retrieve all screens from Broadsign.

The list may be further refined using the last_modified date, which will cause the API to
return only those screens that were modified after the specified date.

methods.name: rbx.clients.broadsign.BroadsignClient.update_creative
documentation:
Update a Creative object.

Note that updating requires the entity_id, which is the ID of the Creative in the supplier
system.

Client for Broadsign Reach APIs.

The API documentation can be found on Google Drive:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/17APVeWVlVLf4T8tqXiexWzfaVnh0U9Oo/view
